text1: Experience the cutting-edge of hair restoration technology with robotic hair transplant in Turkey. As a leading destination for hair transplantation, Turkey offers unparalleled expertise, affordability, and success rates. Robotic hair transplant is becoming popular globally due to its precision and quality. It also works faster thus minimizing procedure time without compromising on results. At <color>Asli Tarcan</color>, we provide robotic-assisted procedures like FUE, FUT, and DHI, achieving natural-looking results with minimal downtime. Discover the benefits of robotic hair transplants in Turkey, from expert surgeons to state-of-the-art clinics, and transform your hair loss concerns into a luscious, full head of hair. We will cover everything related to robotic hair transplant in Turkey including process, benefits, post-op recovery and cost. So, let us get started!
text2: Robotic hair transplant is a state-of-the-art procedure that involves robotics and artificial intelligence to transplant hair follicles from the donor area to the recipient area. Generally, this process involves a robotic arm and AI technology to deliver required outcomes. Further, AI instructs arms to extract the best grafts from the area and implant with precision using angle that will provide the maximum growth. Robotic hair transplant is the future of hair restoration. Since, it has successfully delivered high-quality results, saving time without errors. At our hair transplant clinic in Turkey, we have proudly delivered robotic assisted hair transplants to numerous satisfied customers. The latest medical innovation is being used with our patent-pending automated hair transplant system.
title2: Devices used in Robotic Hair Transplant
text3: Advancements in robotic hair transplant have led to the development of robots that assist doctors in the procedure. However, two robotic hair restoration systems are the most popular due to their results and precision. Here we discussed both these robotics systems in detail. ARTAS It is an advanced technique offering unmatched efficiency and precision. The ARTAS robot extracts and implants hair from the donor to recipient areas. It was developed by an American company in 2008. Further, the machine is popular for its safety and digital cameras that provide 3D vision of the follicles. Thus supporting the right selection of implants. The latest ARTAS system was made available in 2018, which performs the complete surgery on its own. NeoGraft NeoGraft was the first company to launch robotics for hair transplant in 2009. The robot would use a compressed micro-punch system that sucks out each hair follicle. Further, it also offers speed-controlling features. Moreover, the robot also assists in analyzing skin and selecting right grafting. The surgical team can also limit its operations depending on the requirement, while performing the rest of tasks by themselves like FUE or DHI.
title3: Difference between FUE, DHI, FUT and IFA
text4:
There are many different techniques for hair transplantation. Therefore, it is important to learn about the key differences between these and compare them with robotic hair transplant system.

This comparison will give an insight into why robotic hair transplant is better than all other procedures.

Here are details and differences between hair transplant processes available at Aslı Tarcan.

<header>Follicular Unit Extraction (FUE)</header>
It is the most common and classic hair transplant procedure that can be performed either manually (by hand) or with robotic assistance. Moreover, it is applicable to both the entire hair transplantation procedure (extraction and implantation) or only to the implantation phase.

<header>Follicular Unit Transplantation (FUT)</header>
It is the oldest hair transplantation technique, that uses a strip of scalp (10–25 cm) from the cranial occiput. While, follicles are extracted after this strip is replanted on the hairlines while closing the donor area with sutures or staples. However, it was reduced as it was painful and left scars.

<header>Direct Hair Implantation (DHI)</header>
It is a modern technique, which uses a Choi pen to extract and re-implant the follicles instantly. Thus, making it the least invasive transplantation technique. However, it requires great expertise and dexterity on part of the surgeon.

<header>Robotic Hair Transplant</header>
Robotic hair transplant is known as Automatic Follicular Implantation (IFA) uses robots to automatically analyze, select, suck and implant. It is the most advanced technique and has delivered high quality results with zero scars and pain.
text5:
Before visiting a hair clinic in Turkey for implantation, you should know whether you are an ideal candidate for this procedure or not. It will help you save time and cost.

Although automated hair transplant in Turkey is non-invasive, yet there are certain conditions that bar individuals from adopting this procedure. Here are individuals who are perfect candidates to proceed with a robotic hair transplant:

• Men and women experiencing pattern baldness, hair thinning or receding hairline (androgenetic alopecia, alopecia areata, traction alopecia and traumatic hair loss)
• Individuals who have a sufficient donor hair density, thickness and texture
• Patients looking for natural-looking hair restoration method
• Although it depend case to case, but 25–65 years age is ideal
• You do not have an active hair loss or shedding
• Have a flexible scalp for support
• Have realistic expectation
• Bears an overall healthy condition
text6: Robotic hair transplantation is a procedure with minimal surgical intervention. Unlike other transplant processes, there is no involvement of surgical scalpels. However, the robot performs the procedure under the supervision of our specialized doctors. The general steps involved are almost all the same as in classic transplantation. However, the involvement of robotic machines slightly differs from other methodologies. The common steps involved in robotic FUE include the following: Step 1: Consultation and Preparation (1.5 hour) Consultation is important to set clear goals and expectations. Further, the patient’s head is shaved with a robotic program and a sample of hair with a length of 1mm is selected for scan. Robot would then analyze the sample and provide feedback regarding further procedure. If you are fit for the transplant, our team would prepare you for the procedure. It includes washing and cleaning the scalp and administering local anesthesia. Step 2: Robotic Hair Harvesting (1–2 hours) Robotics systems such as ARTAS or NeoGraft will harvest the donor area. Since these are advanced tools, thus ensure precision in extraction of the right follicles. The doctor would provide skin tension on the surgical area allowing the robotic system to determine follicles’ location accurately. Once completed, donor hair is stored in specialized containers to keep their natural health intact. Up to 2000–2500 roots can be harvested in a single session. Step 3: Recipient Site Creation (2 hours) Robotic hair restoration machine will prepare recipient sites according to your initial assessment. It will prepare tailored site based on individual growth patterns and develop angles and directions for optimized growth. Step 4: Graft Placement (1–2 hours) Once the site is ready, the system would start harvesting grafts into recipient areas. It will again use high-end methodologies to ensure precision for natural-looking results. Likewise, robots place graft on areas, which are best suited for hair growth based on its assessment. Since, it has a 3D optical system that enables it to select these follicles optimally. Step 5: Finishing (30 minutes) After implantation, it will apply topical antibiotics and creams as a post-op care. Your doctor would then take over the proceedings by giving recovery instructions and schedule follow-up appointments.
title6: Post Robotic Hair Transplant Care and Recovery
text7:
Although robotic hair transplant in Turkey is a non-incisional procedure, yet you need to take care of post-op to maximize the output. Further, you can resume your routine within 2–3 days of the procedure.

Following these instructions will help you reduce downtime and improve recovery chances. Here are general guidelines to follow a successful robotic FUE hair transplant:

• Rest with head elevated (45° angle)
• Avoid heavy lifting, bending, or strenuous activities
• No driving for 24 hours
• Avoid smoking, alcohol, or caffeine
• Avoid direct sun exposure
• Keep scalp and grafts moist with gentle spray
• Apply topical antibiotics and creams as directed
• Avoid tight hair styling
• Avoid direct water pressure on grafts
• Pat dry, don’t rub
• Ensure follow-up appointments to keep track of your recovery
text8: While the robots perform hair transplantation, surgeons can oversee the working. Thus allowing precision at two levels. Moreover, the combination of tech and AI has delivered a high success rate. Additionally, robots are capable of reducing procedure time while collecting the best hair for the surgery. Here are key advantages of hair replacement through robotics: 1 – Easy Consultation Since robots use algorithms, hence they can make the best decisions during the consultation phase. They have successfully tested and reported the ideal angle of growth after hair transplant. Thus significantly assisting the surgeon’s work. 2 – Speed Robots have reduced implantation time by 50%, thus reducing fatigue of both the patient and medical team. 3 – Cost-Effective Transplant cost has reduced by 30% as robots have reduced manual intervention. This is amazing, as more individuals have started opting for robotic assisted transplantation because of its high value for money. 4 – High Precision Since the procedure is non-invasive and painless, automated extraction provides precision. Further, it also reduces trauma to capillary dermis and leaves no scars. 5 – Better Results Since the waiting time of hair grafts between extraction and implantation has reduced, thus the regrowth chances have increased by 97%.
title8: Who is not fit for Robotic Hair Transplant?
text9: Robotic hair transplant is not for all, thus individuals with certain medical conditions are not suited for this procedure. Further, individuals with specific hair loss conditions also do not make the cut for this type of process. Here are constraints for the robotics hair transplant: - Active scalp conditions (psoriasis, eczema, etc.) - Uncontrolled diabetes - Hypertension (uncontrolled) - Bleeding disorders (hemophilia, etc.) - Autoimmune disorders (lupus, rheumatoid arthritis, etc.) - Active hair loss or shedding - Insufficient donor hair - Under 25 years old (hair loss may still progress) - Over 65 years old (hair loss may be too extensive) - Previous hair transplant complications - Lack of understanding of procedure limitations
title9: Side Effects of Robotic Hair Transplant
text10: While robotic hair transplant is generally considered a safe procedure, there are few side effects that may affect post-op. However, these are not severe and should go-away within two weeks of transplantation. Nevertheless, if any of below mentioned side effects persists for longer than expected, immediately consult our experts for feedback on solutions. Below is a list of common side effects: - Swelling - Itching - Redness - Numbness or tingling - Mild pain or discomfort
text11: How long does robotic hair transplant last? Since this method uses its own hair for grafting, thus it can last for a lifetime. However, it also depends on the health of the donor area and post-op care adopted by the patient. What is the best robotic hair transplant? ARTAS system is considered the best for its smaller incision, reduced trauma and high quality graft selection. What is the difference between robotic and FUE? The major difference between a robotic and FUE hair transplant is that the latter is a completely manual, hands on procedure performed by a skilled surgeon. Whereas, robotic system involve AI and robots to perform the same task. What is the failure rate of robotic surgery? Failure rate of robotic surgery is minimal and a study shows only 0.38% surgeries fail. However, surgeon’s supervision is mandatory while performing robotic hair transplant. How painful is robotic surgery? Most people would only experience a slight pain while recovering from a robotic hair transplant. Thus, making it an almost painless procedure. How long does robotic hair surgery take to show results? It will take around 3–6 months after the robotic hair transplant that most patients will start seeing the results.
